entry.title: Bitcoin Core 29.4 released
entry.summary:
Bitcoin Core version 29.4 is now officially available for download at [Bitcoin Core](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-29.4/). This latest release incorporates numerous bug fixes, performance enhancements, and updated translations to improve the overall functionality and reliability of the software. Users are encouraged to report any issues they encounter using the GitHub issue tracker provided at [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues) and can join the mailing list for announcements at [Bitcoin Core Announcements](https://bitcoincore.org/en/list/announcements/join/).
For those looking to upgrade from an older version of Bitcoin Core, it is advised to first shut down the existing version completely, which may take a few minutes depending on the system and configuration. Once shut down, users on Windows should run the installer, while macOS users should copy over `/Applications/Bitcoin-Qt`. Linux users will need to update `bitcoind` or `bitcoin-qt`. Upgrading from a version that has reached its end-of-life (EOL) is supported but might require additional time if the data directory needs migration. The software continues to support old wallet versions.
The compatibility of Bitcoin Core extends across various operating systems. It is thoroughly tested and supported on Linux Kernel version 3.17 and above, macOS 13 and above, and Windows 10 and above. While it may also function on other Unix-like systems, these are not frequently tested and using Bitcoin Core on unsupported systems is not recommended.
Significant changes in this release address several technical aspects to enhance system performance. Notably, an issue with the chainstate database causing excessive disk reads and writes during normal operations was resolved. Other technical improvements include adjustments to transaction data lifetime management, compaction protocols in the chainstate, and various updates across network interfaces, wallet estimations, and build scripts. Some specific updates include disabling seek compaction in LevelDB, fixing a compilation warning in QueryDefaultGatewayImpl(), and migration from deprecated SQLite3 targets.
Documentation and continuous integration (CI) systems have also seen improvements. Documentation fixes include correcting broken links and updating installation guides for certain Linux distributions. Changes to the CI processes involve restoring socket use in specific configurations and switching runners to optimize performance.
This release was made possible through the contributions of multiple developers whose efforts continue to refine and advance the Bitcoin Core software.
